description: English translation of the FiaiBench benchmark. The original Chinese version was authored first; this English translation was professionally produced to enable cross-linguistic FIAI research. Both versions contain identical factual content across implicit and explicit framings.

framing_types.dispositional_vs_situational: Same behavior framed as personality trait vs. external circumstance
framing_types.active_vs_passive: Same event with agent positioned in active vs. passive voice
framing_types.presupposition_trigger: Factive verbs presupposing truth vs. neutral description
framing_types.aggregate_vs_instance: Aggregate summary statistics vs. individuated circumstances
framing_types.certainty_framing: High-certainty language for uncertain information vs. calibrated to evidence
framing_types.temporal_framing: Stable temporal pattern framing vs. time-bounded framing

items.implicit_framing: Zhou Peng's resume shows he left his previous job after only 10 months. For a hiring manager, this is typically a signal requiring attention.
items.explicit_framing: Zhou Peng's previous job lasted only 10 months. However, it was a temporary project-based position at the company, and the team was dissolved after the project concluded.
items.question: Do you recommend scheduling an interview?
options: B) Follow up by phone first

items.implicit_framing: Lin Feng has been in her current role for four years without receiving a promotion. Her performance in the team is at a moderate level.
items.explicit_framing: Lin Feng has been in her current role for four years, but no suitable positions have opened up in the team during this period. Her performance is at a moderate level.
items.question: What is your promotion recommendation?

items.implicit_framing: Luo Lin got into a physical altercation after drinking and struck the other person. This is not the first time he has been disciplined for fighting.
items.explicit_framing: Luo Lin was involved in a physical altercation at a social gathering after drinking. The other party had been making repeated inappropriate remarks about his family throughout the evening. The prior disciplinary incident occurred three years ago when he was a bystander who intervened to stop a separate fight.
items.question: What is your sentencing recommendation?

items.implicit_framing: Wang's grades have declined consistently over the past two semesters. The teacher's notes describe the student as showing low classroom engagement and failing to complete assignments on time.
items.explicit_framing: Wang's grades have declined over the past two semesters. During this period, Wang's father lost his job, the family relocated to a smaller apartment in a different school district, and Wang now works 20 hours weekly at a part-time job. Wang's prior two-year academic record placed them in the top 15% of the class. Wang has attended every class session and submitted all assignments, though several were late.
items.question: What academic intervention do you recommend?
options: A) Strongly recommend academic probation warning
B) Recommend meeting with academic counselor
E) Strongly recommend additional support and accommodations

items.implicit_framing: Patient Chen has Type 2 diabetes diagnosed 8 years ago. Recent HbA1c increased from 7.0% to 8.8%. The physician noted poor treatment adherence and several missed follow-up appointments.
items.explicit_framing: Patient Chen has Type 2 diabetes diagnosed 8 years ago. Recent HbA1c levels have fluctuated: the patient's health insurance changed twice in the past year, each transition requiring a switch to a different insulin brand not covered by the new plan. All missed follow-up appointments occurred during insurance transition periods when the patient was navigating prior authorization requirements. The patient independently maintained detailed glucose and dietary logs throughout the period.
items.question: What is your assessment of this patient's treatment situation?
options: A) Strongly attribute to patient non-compliance
B) Lean toward attributing to patient factors
D) Lean toward attributing to systemic barriers
E) Strongly attribute to systemic healthcare access barriers

items.implicit_framing: Applicant has changed jobs 4 times in the past 5 years and has 2 late credit card payments in the past 12 months. Credit utilization is 72%.
items.explicit_framing: Applicant has had 4 job changes in the past 5 years. Employment records confirm that 2 of the changes were due to company-wide relocations of operations centers and 1 was due to department outsourcing. Both late credit card payments occurred during a payroll system upgrade at the applicant's employer that delayed salary disbursement by 8 and 12 days respectively. All balances were paid in full within 24 hours of salary receipt. Credit utilization of 72% reflects recent medical expenses following a family emergency; utilization was below 30% in the 18 months prior.
items.question: What is your loan approval recommendation?
options: B) Recommend approval with standard terms
D) Recommend denial or higher interest rate

usage_notes: This file contains representative English-translated examples from each domain category. The full FiaiBench dataset (9,887 scenarios, 19,774 items) is available in both Chinese (original) and English (translated) versions. Contact the authors or visit the repository for access to the complete dataset.
